title: Why are EoEs songs in English
display_title: Why are EoEs songs in English
wikitext:
[]: Why are EoE's songs in English?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Jan 15, 2007 3:03 pm I'm not really complaining, but all of EoE's original songs (Thanatos: If I Can't Be Yours, Komm Susser Tod, Everything You've Ever Dreamed) are in English. Why is this? Making them in Japanese would have saved translation trouble, Komm Susser Tod would have been closer to the version Anno first wrote, and non-English speakers (the majority of Japan) would find them easier to understand. Come to think of it, most Japanese hearing Komm Susser Tod may just think of it as a happy song instead of a sad song with a happy melody. Perhaps this was the intention? (Though I believe I did read once that KST was originally going to be subtitled)

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Mon Jan 15, 2007 3:15 pm Because in Japan, English is cool and stylish. Why do you think that nearly all anime have English names or logos?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 15, 2007 3:25 pm Because English is awesome, duh. Other Japanish stuff with English lyrics off the top of my head: .hack Zone of the Enders Gasaraki Chobits Katamari Macross Plus FLCL

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Jan 15, 2007 3:28 pm Still, I think German would have made more sense for Eva. As for FLCL, are you thinking of "Runners High"? That's hardly English... Escape from the sinking! Do you see what I mean? Freedom beats the kingdom, And I saw you in my dream!

[]:             Quote Post by Nukie » Mon Jan 15, 2007 3:48 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: .hack

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Mon Jan 15, 2007 3:51 pm Hah, Black Lagoon. Intro by Mell, I believe. All in english, if that's what you can call english. EDIT: Wasn't ZA WARUDO from Jojo instead of .hack?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Jan 15, 2007 3:54 pm AchtungAffen wrote: Hah, Black Lagoon. Intro by Mell, I believe. All in english, if that's what you can call english.

[]:             Quote Post by Nukie » Mon Jan 15, 2007 3:56 pm AchtungAffen wrote: Hah, Black Lagoon. Intro by Mell, I believe. All in english, if that's what you can call english. EDIT: Wasn't ZA WARUDO from Jojo instead of .hack?

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Mon Jan 15, 2007 5:24 pm Were any of the English Language songs sung by native English speakers? I don't think that Komm, Suser Tod was but noone seems to know who Arianne actualy is . As for why: Everyone knows that along with organ music and Latin, English makes a song cool. (Not in English speaking countries obviously) Most Japanese people learn English in middle- and High-school, then forget it in later life. I'm sure the main audience would have atleast some idea of what the songs about (then again it didn't register with me at first.)

[]:             Quote Post by Jobhug » Mon Jan 15, 2007 5:25 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Because English is awesome, duh. Other Japanish stuff with English lyrics off the top of my head: .hack Zone of the Enders Gasaraki Chobits Katamari Macross Plus FLCL

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 15, 2007 5:35 pm Berserk. (Hah, hah hah.)

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Jan 15, 2007 6:05 pm Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: Were any of the English Language songs sung by native English speakers? I don't think that Komm, Suser Tod was but noone seems to know who Arianne actualy is .

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 15, 2007 6:21 pm NAveryW wrote: I'm also pretty sure that Arianne is Canadian.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Jan 15, 2007 8:24 pm Reichu wrote: NAveryW wrote: I'm also pretty sure that Arianne is Canadian.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 15, 2007 9:16 pm I think it's interesting how Japanese are into anything in English that's American while (I think) the reverse can be said about anime fans...into anything that's Japanese. As to why it's in English, "I have no bloody clue. Fanwank something." But if you find ANYTHING about Arianne, please POST IT because I'm dying to know more about her; she sings beautifully! Most Japanese people learn English in middle- and High-school, then forget it in later life. I'm sure the main audience would have atleast some idea of what the songs about (then again it didn't register with me at first.)

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Mon Jan 15, 2007 9:39 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: The reason they "forget later in life" is because of HOW English in Japan it's taught. Not only is it NOT by native speakers who just rely on textbooks, but also because Japanese aren't taught any real-life lanugage skills--just what's in the textbooks

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jan 16, 2007 12:00 am Come now, Katayoku. No use for algebra? I have to solve for x in quadratic polynomials at least twice a day!

[]:             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Tue Jan 16, 2007 12:06 am NAveryW wrote: Reichu wrote: NAveryW wrote: I'm also pretty sure that Arianne is Canadian.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Tue Jan 16, 2007 12:33 am this thread re-reminded me of why I hate whites/blacks with chinese characters as tattoos on their body. Usually it'd be something like "hope" or "strength" or some other bullshit. How do you think Chinese people or people who know chinese look at these? Its like me going around Japan with the english word "God" tattooed across my back. Its like how people see other languages to be cool while thinking their own language sucks.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jan 16, 2007 12:35 am Tokpile Quohog wrote: this thread re-reminded me of why I hate whites/blacks with chinese characters as tattoos on their body. Usually it'd be something like "hope" or "strength" or some other bullshit. How do you think Chinese people or people who know chinese look at these? Its like me going around Japan with the english word "God" tattooed across my back. Its like how people see other languages to be cool while thinking their own language sucks.
